site.title: iLuvBnMe
site.tagline: Be Big. Be Bold. Be You.

posts.title: A Love Letter to the Woman You're Growing Into
posts.excerpt: There is a version of you that already exists on the other side of this season. She is kinder with herself and surer of her yes. And here is the quiet secret: you don't have to reach her to be worthy of your own love right now. You already are her, just earlier in the story.

body.x: There is a version of you that already exists on the other side of this season. She is kinder with herself and surer of her yes. And here is the quiet secret: you don't have to reach her to be worthy of your own love right now. You already are her, just earlier in the story.

We are taught to treat ourselves like projects. Fix the flaw, close the gap, arrive at the finished, polished self, and then, finally, rest. But the finished self is a mirage. There is always another horizon. If love waits at the finish line, love waits forever.
The middle is holy

The messy middle, the unsure, in-between, still-figuring-it-out middle, is not a waiting room. It is the whole point. It is where the roots grow. Nothing beautiful has ever bloomed by skipping the part underground, in the dark, growing.
You are not behind. You are growing, and growth has no deadline.

When you pull a card in the middle of it all, notice how rarely the cards scold you. They meet you where you are. The Star does not ask why you aren't further along; she simply pours her water and shows you that hope is a renewable resource. Let that be your posture toward yourself, too.
A small practice

body.intro: Tonight, before the day folds closed, try this:
items: Place a hand on your heart and name one way you grew today, however quiet.
Say aloud: "I am allowed to be a work in progress and a masterpiece at once."
Pull a single card and let it be a blessing, not a verdict.

body.x: You are allowed to love yourself in the unfinished draft. In fact, that is the only place love has ever lived: in the real, ongoing, gloriously in-progress you.

posts.title: How to Read Your First Tarot Spread
posts.excerpt: The deck is not a test. This is the first thing I want you to know before you ever turn a card. You will not be graded on how many meanings you memorized, and the cards are not waiting to catch you getting it wrong. A tarot spread is a conversation, and you already speak the language.

body.x: The deck is not a test. This is the first thing I want you to know before you ever turn a card. You will not be graded on how many meanings you memorized, and the cards are not waiting to catch you getting it wrong. A tarot spread is a conversation, and you already speak the language.

When I laid out my very first spread, I was terrified I would "do it wrong." I had the little booklet open, my eyes darting between the card and the paragraph, certain the real meaning was somewhere on the page and not somewhere in me. It took me years to learn the truth: the booklet is training wheels. Your intuition was always the bicycle.
Before you touch the cards

Reading begins before you shuffle. Find a pocket of quiet. Light a candle if you have one, not for magic exactly, but because a small flame tells your nervous system it is safe to slow down. Take three slow breaths. Then ask your question, out loud if you can.
Make it an open question, not a yes-or-no. Instead of "Will I get the job?" try "What do I need to know as I step toward this work?" The cards are far more generous with open doors than closed ones.
A simple three-card spread
Forget the elaborate ten-card layouts for now. Your first spread has three cards, and you get to decide what each position means. Here is a gentle, beginner-friendly version to start with.

body.intro: Shuffle until it simply feels like enough, then draw three cards and lay them left to right:
items: Where you are — the energy of this moment, exactly as it is.
What is asking for your attention — the lesson, the block, or the invitation.
What supports you — the medicine, the next small step, the gift already in your hands.

body.x: The cards don't tell your future. They show you the present so clearly that the future changes.
Read with your body, not your memory

Turn the first card and, before you reach for any meaning, notice your body. Does your chest tighten or soften? Does the image pull you in or push you away? That flicker is data. That flicker is you, already reading.

Then look at the picture the way you would describe a dream to a friend. Who is in it? What are they doing? What is the weather? A figure turning away might be a goodbye, or a moment of rest, or a quiet refusal, and only you know which one it is today. The keyword in the booklet is a starting point, never the last word.
When you get stuck

You will draw a card that means nothing to you. Everyone does. Do not panic and do not force it. Ask the card a follow-up out loud, "What are you trying to show me?", and pull one more to clarify. Or simply sit with the image and let it work on you overnight. Meaning often arrives late, and always right on time.
Close the way you opened: a breath, a thank-you, a candle blown out. You did it. You read your first spread, and I promise the second one is even sweeter.

posts.title: Your Saturn Return, Made Simple
posts.excerpt: Somewhere around your twenty-ninth birthday, the ground may start to feel less like ground. Relationships you thought were solid reveal their cracks. The job that once fit begins to itch. You catch yourself asking, out of nowhere, "Wait, is this actually my life, or one I inherited?" If any of this sounds familiar, welcome. You are likely in your Saturn return.

body.x: Somewhere around your twenty-ninth birthday, the ground may start to feel less like ground. Relationships you thought were solid reveal their cracks. The job that once fit begins to itch. You catch yourself asking, out of nowhere, "Wait, is this actually my life, or one I inherited?" If any of this sounds familiar, welcome. You are likely in your Saturn return.
What is actually happening

Saturn takes about twenty-nine and a half years to travel all the way around the sky and return to the exact spot it occupied on the day you were born. When it arrives back home, it knocks on your door. This is your first Saturn return, and it usually lands somewhere between the ages of twenty-eight and thirty-one.

Astrologically, Saturn is the taskmaster, the architect, the wise and slightly stern elder of the zodiac. He is not cruel. He is committed. Saturn's whole job is to ask you one question in a hundred different ways: what are you building, and is it built on something true?
Why it feels like an earthquake

Anything in your life that is not structurally sound tends to shake loose during this time. The friendship that runs on obligation. The career you chose to make someone else proud. The version of yourself you performed because it kept you safe. Saturn does not topple these things to punish you. He loosens what was never load-bearing so you can rebuild on rock instead of sand.
Saturn does not come to break you. He comes to find out what in you cannot be broken.
How to work with it, not against it

body.intro: You cannot skip your Saturn return, but you can meet it with grace. A few ways to work with the teacher instead of fighting him:
items: Get honest about what is yours. Ask of each big commitment: did I choose this, or did I absorb it? Keep what is true. Grieve what is not.
Build one small structure. Saturn loves foundations. A savings habit, a morning routine, a boundary you actually hold. Small and consistent beats grand and abandoned.
Let discipline be a love language. The work you do now is a gift to the woman you will be at thirty-five. Do it slowly. Do it for her.

body.x: The years of your Saturn return can feel lonely, heavy, and strangely clarifying all at once. But on the other side, most people report the same thing: for the first time, their life feels like their own. The shaking was never the end of your foundation. It was the pouring of it.

posts.title: The Moon Ritual I Return to Again and Again
posts.excerpt: I have tried the elaborate rituals. The ones with seven crystals arranged by the phase of the moon, the printed correspondences, the incense I could never quite identify. They were beautiful, and I never kept them up. The ritual that actually stayed with me for years is almost embarrassingly simple: a candle, a card, and a single question.

body.x: I have tried the elaborate rituals. The ones with seven crystals arranged by the phase of the moon, the printed correspondences, the incense I could never quite identify. They were beautiful, and I never kept them up. The ritual that actually stayed with me for years is almost embarrassingly simple: a candle, a card, and a single question.

I come back to it on new moons and full moons, on ordinary Tuesdays when I feel unmoored, whenever I have drifted a little too far from myself. It takes ten minutes. It asks for almost nothing. And it has quietly rearranged my relationship with my own mind.
The ritual

body.intro: Here it is, in full. Steal it, change it, make it entirely your own:
items: Light one candle. Put your phone in another room. Let the small flame be the only urgent thing.
Take three breaths and drop the day. Feel your shoulders come down from your ears. You are off the clock now.
Ask one question. New moon: "What am I planting?" Full moon: "What am I ready to release?" Any night at all: "What do I need to hear?"
Pull a single card. Do not reach for the book. Look at the image and let it answer in its own language.
Write three sentences. Not an essay. Just what the card stirred, and one thing you will carry into tomorrow.
Thank the card. Blow out the candle. That is it. You are done.

body.x: A ritual is just love, repeated. It does not have to be elaborate to be holy.

The magic was never in the candle or the card. It is in the ten minutes I give myself, on purpose, to listen. The ritual is a door I keep walking through, and every time, some quieter, truer part of me is waiting on the other side.

posts.title: On Sovereignty: Choosing Yourself Out Loud
posts.excerpt: For most of my life I thought choosing myself was something you did quietly, apologetically, with a long explanation attached. I could rest, but only after I had earned it and reassured everyone that I still loved them. I could say no, but only if I softened it into a maybe first. Sovereignty taught me something different. Sovereignty says you are allowed to choose yourself out loud.

body.x: For most of my life I thought choosing myself was something you did quietly, apologetically, with a long explanation attached. I could rest, but only after I had earned it and reassured everyone that I still loved them. I could say no, but only if I softened it into a maybe first. Sovereignty taught me something different. Sovereignty says you are allowed to choose yourself out loud.
Sovereignty is not selfishness

Let us clear this up first, because the fear of being selfish keeps so many good women small. Selfishness takes from others to fill yourself. Sovereignty fills yourself so you have something real to give. One is a taking. The other is a homecoming. When you belong to yourself first, everyone who loves you gets the whole person, not the leftovers.
Sovereignty is the quiet, radical act of belonging to yourself before you belong to anyone else.
Out loud is the practice

The "out loud" part is where it gets brave. It is easy to choose yourself in secret. The growth is in letting people see it. Saying "that does not work for me" without a paragraph of justification. Naming what you want before someone has to guess. Taking your seat at the table instead of hovering near the wall, hoping to be invited.

People may be surprised at first, especially the ones who benefited from your smallness. That surprise is not a sign you have done something wrong. It is the sound of an old agreement being renegotiated in your favor.
A practice in sovereignty

body.intro: You do not reclaim your sovereignty in one grand gesture. You do it in small, repeated choices:
items: Say one true no this week, cleanly, with no over-explaining. Let the sentence end where it ends.
Voice one want out loud before anyone asks. "I would love to..." is a complete and worthy sentence.
Notice where you shrink to keep the peace, and stay your full size for ten uncomfortable seconds longer.

body.x: You will not get it perfect, and you do not need to. Sovereignty is not a fortress you build once. It is a muscle you use, a door you keep choosing to walk through as your whole, unapologetic self. Choose yourself. And this time, let them hear you do it.

posts.title: Mercury Retrograde Without the Panic
posts.excerpt: Three or four times a year, the internet loses its mind. Do not sign the contract. Do not text your ex. Do not buy the laptop. Mercury is retrograde, and apparently we are all doomed. I want to offer you a calmer, truer story, because Mercury retrograde is not a punishment. It is an invitation, and once you learn to read it, you might even start to look forward to it.

body.x: Three or four times a year, the internet loses its mind. Do not sign the contract. Do not text your ex. Do not buy the laptop. Mercury is retrograde, and apparently we are all doomed. I want to offer you a calmer, truer story, because Mercury retrograde is not a punishment. It is an invitation, and once you learn to read it, you might even start to look forward to it.
What is really going on up there

Mercury is not actually moving backward. From our view here on Earth, the planet only appears to slow, pause, and drift in reverse, the way a slower train seems to roll backward when a faster one passes it. It is an optical illusion with a very real feeling attached. Mercury rules communication, technology, travel, and the way our minds process information, so when it appears to stall, those areas can feel a little scrambled.
The invitation hidden in the mess

Notice that almost every helpful Mercury retrograde word starts with "re." Review. Revise. Reflect. Reconnect. Rest. This is not a season for launching the brand new; it is a season for returning to the unfinished. The half-written project, the friend you meant to call, the boundary you meant to reinforce. The sky is asking you to slow down and go back for what you left behind.
Three times a year, the universe hands you a bookmark and says: go back and finish reading.
How to move through it

body.intro: You do not have to hide indoors for three weeks. Just adjust your posture:
items: Build in buffer. Leave early, back up your files, read the email twice before you hit send. Friction is likely; margin absorbs it.
Choose re- over new. Edit the draft instead of starting three more. Tend what already exists.
Say what you mean. Misunderstandings bloom now, so trade assumptions for one honest, direct sentence.

body.x: And the classic warnings? There is a kernel of truth in them. It is simply a wobbly time for fresh starts and final decisions, so give big commitments a little extra breathing room. But your ex texting you back has nothing to do with the planets and everything to do with the fact that they miss you. Some things, the stars leave entirely up to you.

posts.title: The Star Card & Finding Hope After Hard Seasons
posts.excerpt: In the tarot, the Star always comes right after the Tower. That is not an accident, and if you know the Tower, you know exactly why it matters. The Tower is the card of sudden collapse, the lightning strike, the thing you thought was permanent crumbling in a single afternoon. And there, on the very next page of the deck, is the Star. The message is built into the order: after the worst of it, hope.

body.x: In the tarot, the Star always comes right after the Tower. That is not an accident, and if you know the Tower, you know exactly why it matters. The Tower is the card of sudden collapse, the lightning strike, the thing you thought was permanent crumbling in a single afternoon. And there, on the very next page of the deck, is the Star. The message is built into the order: after the worst of it, hope.
What the Star shows us

Picture her. A woman kneels at the edge of a pool beneath a wide night sky, one foot on the land and one in the water. She holds two vessels and pours them gently, one onto the earth and one back into the pool, nothing wasted, everything returned. Above her, a single bright star is surrounded by seven smaller ones. She is undressed, unguarded, unhurried. She has nothing left to hide and nothing left to prove.
The Star does not promise the storm never happened. She promises you survived it, and softened instead of hardened.
Hope as a practice, not a feeling

We tend to think of hope as something that happens to us, a mood that arrives once things improve. The Star teaches the opposite. Hope is something you tend, like a small flame you cup your hands around on a windy night. It is a choice you make before the evidence is in. Some mornings, faith is simply getting up and pouring the water again.
Tending your own small flame

body.intro: When you are crawling out of a hard season, do not rush to feel radiant. Just tend the flame:
items: Name one thing that survived. A friendship, a value, a version of you that made it through. Hope grows from what is still standing.
Do one gentle, restorative thing daily. Water, sleep, sunlight, a walk. The Star heals through the ordinary, not the dramatic.
Let yourself be seen, undefended, by one safe person. Her whole gift is that she finally stopped hiding.

body.x: If the Star has found you, take it as the quiet promise it is: the hardest part is behind you. You do not have to feel fully healed to begin again. You only have to pour the water, look up at the light, and trust that morning is already on its way.

episodes.title: Full Moon in Aquarius: Releasing Who You Were
episodes.description: The full moon asks a hard question: who are you still carrying that you were never meant to keep? A reading and a ritual for letting go.
showNotes: The astrology of this Aquarius full moon and what it is stirring up in all of us.
Why release is not the same thing as forgetting.
A guided full-moon releasing ritual you can do tonight with one candle and a scrap of paper.
The card of the week: the Eight of Cups, and the brave, quiet exit.

transcript.x: [Cold open — quiet, no music]
Who are you still carrying that you were never meant to keep? Sit with that for a second. Don't answer fast. We'll come back to it.
[Theme music swells 6 seconds, then settles underneath]

Hey love, and welcome back to iLuvBnMe, the podcast where we read the cards, the stars, and ourselves a little more gently. I'm Stefy, and if you're listening the night this drops, there is a big, bright, slightly rebellious full moon in Aquarius hanging over us. So grab your tea, get cozy, and let's talk about what she is here to help us release.
Segment one — what this full moon is actually doing

Okay, quick sky report, and I promise to keep it human. A full moon is always a moment of culmination. Whatever seeds you planted at the last new moon, this is where they come up for air. And in Aquarius, the sign of the rebel, the visionary, the one who refuses to belong to the wrong crowd, the theme is liberation. Aquarius doesn't ask "how do I fit in?" She asks "who am I when I stop performing?"
So if the last few weeks have felt itchy, if you've been quietly outgrowing a room you used to love, that is not you being difficult. That is the moon doing her work.
Segment two — release is not forgetting

I want to gently correct something, because I hear this a lot. Releasing someone or something does not mean it didn't matter. It doesn't mean you forget. Release just means you stop asking a chapter that already ended to keep paying your rent. You are allowed to be grateful for who you were at twenty-three and still set her down. She got you here. She doesn't have to drive anymore.
You can honor a season and still let it end.
Segment three — the ritual
So here is what we're going to do tonight. This is my go-to full-moon releasing ritual, and it costs nothing.
[Music drops out for the ritual — just voice]

Find a quiet spot and a small piece of paper. Light a candle. Take three slow breaths with me: in through the nose, and a long sigh out. Now, on that paper, write down the thing you are ready to release. Not what you think you should release. The real one. The name, the habit, the story you keep telling about yourself that isn't true anymore.

When you're ready, read it once, out loud, and then say: "Thank you. I'm setting this down now." And safely, over a sink or a bowl, burn the paper, or if fire isn't your thing, tear it into small pieces. Watch it change form. That is you, changing form too.
The card of the week

I pulled one card for us this week and it's the Eight of Cups, a figure walking away from a row of cups they built, under a moon, toward the mountains. It's the card of the brave, quiet exit. Of leaving something good because you know there's something truer out there. If that's you right now, consider this your permission slip.
Before you go

If this landed, do me a favor and come sit with the rest of us. The free Self-Love Starter Kit is waiting at the link, and if you want to go deeper, Frequency Shift is where we do this work together, month after month. And if you're feeling generous, share this episode with the friend who's mid-release right now. She'll know who she is.
[Outro music swells]
Until next time, be soft with yourself. You are not losing who you were. You are finally becoming who you are. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: The Empress & the Art of Radical Self-Care
episodes.description: The Empress knows that rest is not a reward. It is your birthright. On slowing down, receiving, and filling your own cup first.
showNotes: Who the Empress is, and why she changed the way I rest.
The real difference between self-care and self-soothing.
A small, uncomfortable practice for learning to receive.
This week's card: the Queen of Pentacles.

transcript.x: [Cold open]
Quick question before we start. When was the last time you rested without earning it first? Just... rested. No to-do list finished, no permission asked. Hold that thought.
[Theme music in, then under]

Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy, and today we are sitting at the feet of my favorite lady in the whole tarot deck: the Empress. She has a lot to teach us about rest, receiving, and the kind of self-care that actually fills you up.
Segment one — meet the Empress

If you've seen her card, you know she is not rushing anywhere. She's reclining in a field of wheat, wrapped in soft fabric, a crown of stars in her hair, everything around her lush and blooming. She is the mother of the deck, the archetype of abundance and creativity. And here's the thing that stopped me in my tracks the first time I really looked at her: she is not producing anything in that image. She is simply receiving. And everything grows anyway.
Segment two — care versus soothing

Let's get honest about self-care, because the word got hijacked. A lot of what we call self-care is actually self-soothing: the glass of wine, the third hour of scrolling, the online cart we fill and never check out. Soothing numbs the feeling. Care tends to the need underneath it. Soothing says "distract me." Care asks "what do you actually need, love?" The Empress is all care. She would never hand you a distraction when what you need is rest.
Rest is not a reward for your productivity. It is the soil everything else grows in.
Segment three — the practice of receiving

So this week I want you to practice the hardest thing for high-functioning, capable women: receiving. Here's your assignment, and it's small. One time this week, when someone offers to help you, say yes. Don't say "oh no, I've got it." Just say "yes, thank you," and let them. Notice how uncomfortable it is. Notice that the world does not end. That discomfort is the exact edge of your growth.
The card of the week

Fittingly, I pulled the Queen of Pentacles for us, the woman who has learned that tending to herself is not separate from tending to her whole abundant life. She keeps a warm home and a full cup at the same time. Let her remind you: you are allowed to be both the giver and the well.
Before you go

If you needed this permission today, it is yours. Grab the free Self-Love Starter Kit at the link, and if you want a whole community practicing this alongside you, come find us inside Frequency Shift. Share this with the friend who's running on empty. Tell her the Empress says lie down.
[Outro music]
Until next time, fill your own cup first. You cannot pour from a pretty, empty thing. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: Human Design 101: Trusting Your Strategy
episodes.description: An easy, loving intro to your energy type and how honoring it changes everything about how you move through the world.
showNotes: A loving, jargon-free intro to what Human Design even is.
The five energy types and how to find yours.
What "strategy" means, and why it changes everything.
A one-week experiment to try your own strategy on for size.

transcript.x: [Cold open]

What if the way you've been told to make decisions, pros and cons lists, thinking it through, being logical, was never actually built for your body? What if you have your own personal user manual, and no one ever handed it to you? That's today.
[Theme music in, under]

Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy, and today is a big, juicy one: Human Design 101. I'm going to give you the loving, no-jargon version, because most intros to this stuff sound like a physics lecture, and you deserve better.
Segment one — what even is Human Design

So, simplest possible version. Human Design is a system that blends a few older frameworks, astrology, the chakras, the I Ching, into one chart based on your exact birth date, time, and place. But you don't need to understand any of the machinery to get value from it. Think of it less like a horoscope and more like a personality map that focuses on one incredibly useful thing: how your energy is actually designed to work.
Segment two — the five types
There are five energy types, and knowing yours is the doorway to everything else. Let me give you the one-line version of each.

Generators and Manifesting Generators, that's most of us, have steady, sustainable life-force energy, and you're designed to respond to life, to light up when the right thing shows up. Projectors are here to guide and to see others deeply; you're not built to grind, you're built to be invited. Manifestors are the initiators, the ones who start things and then let others catch up. And Reflectors, the rarest, are the mirrors, deeply sensitive to the health of the world around them.
If you don't know yours yet, pause this, look up a free Human Design chart, pop in your birth info, and come back. I'll wait.
Segment three — strategy is the whole game

Here's the part that genuinely changed my life. Each type has a "strategy," basically, the right way for you to engage with life so you stop hitting walls. For Generators, it's to wait to respond instead of forcing. For Projectors, it's to wait for the invitation. For Manifestors, it's to inform before you act. For Reflectors, it's to give yourself a full lunar cycle before big decisions.
You were never behind. You were just running on someone else's strategy.
When you start honoring your strategy, the theme is the same for everyone: less resistance. Fewer walls. Things start to feel like they're flowing toward you instead of you dragging them uphill.
Your experiment this week

I don't want you to believe any of this on faith. Human Design is an experiment. So this week, just try your strategy once. If you're a Generator, wait for something to genuinely excite you before you say yes. If you're a Projector, wait to be asked before you offer your brilliant advice. Notice what happens in your body. That is your data.
Before you go

This is the tip of a gorgeous iceberg, and we go so much deeper inside Frequency Shift, including a whole workshop on reading your chart. Grab the free Self-Love Starter Kit at the link to begin, and send this to the friend who's exhausted from doing life the "right" way. Tell her there might be an easier way that's actually hers.
[Outro music]
Until next time, trust your design. You were built on purpose, for a purpose. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: When the Cards Say Wait
episodes.description: Divine timing is real, and so is your impatience. An honest talk on trusting the pause and letting things unfold.
showNotes: The little family of cards that signal divine timing.
Why waiting is an action, not a punishment.
An honest test for telling surrender apart from stalling.
This week's card: the Star.

transcript.x: [Cold open]

You did everything right. You showed up, you did the work, you asked the universe clearly for what you wanted. And still, nothing's moving. Before you decide something is wrong with you, let's talk about what the cards mean when they say: wait.
[Theme music in, under]
Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy, and this one is for everyone in the waiting room of their own life right now. I see you. Let's make the wait mean something.
Segment one — the cards of the pause

There's a little family of cards that show up when it's just not time yet. The Hanged Man, suspended upside down, seeing everything from a new angle because he finally stopped struggling. The Seven of Pentacles, a gardener leaning on his tool, looking at fruit that isn't ripe. The Four of Swords, a figure lying down, resting, not defeated. When these show up, the deck isn't saying no. It's saying not yet, and there's a world of difference.
Segment two — waiting is an action

We treat waiting like it's the absence of doing. But divine timing isn't you sitting frozen while the universe runs late. The wait is the work. The Seven of Pentacles gardener isn't idle; he's letting the roots do what roots do underground. You're allowed to rest in the wait. You're allowed to keep tending. You're just released from the exhausting job of forcing a flower to bloom on your schedule.
Not yet is not the same as no. And rushing a ripening only makes it bitter.
Segment three — surrender or stalling?

Now, real talk, because I don't want you to spiritually bypass your own procrastination. There's a difference between surrender and stalling. Here's my honest test: surrender feels like peace. Stalling feels like avoidance dressed up as patience. If waiting brings you a quiet exhale, trust it. If waiting is actually fear wearing a calm mask, that's your nudge to take the one small brave step you've been avoiding. The cards can say wait. They rarely say hide.
The card of the week

I pulled the Star for us, of course I did, because when you're deep in the wait, she's the one who reminds you the water is still flowing, the light is still on, and this pause is a part of the plan, not a detour from it.
Before you go

If you needed to hear that your timing isn't broken, save this episode and come back to it next time you're white-knuckling the wait. The free Self-Love Starter Kit is at the link, and if you want support while you're in the in-between, Frequency Shift is home. Share this with your friend who's refreshing her inbox for the hundredth time today.
[Outro music]
Until next time, trust the timing of your life. It has never once been late. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: Venus Retrograde & Rewriting Your Worth
episodes.description: Venus turns inward and so do we, revisiting old stories about love, money, and what we believe we deserve.
showNotes: What Venus retrograde actually stirs up in love and money.
The old worth-stories that resurface right about now.
A journaling ritual to catch and rewrite them.
This week's card: the Four of Cups.

transcript.x: [Cold open]

Has an ex crossed your mind out of nowhere this week? Have you found yourself questioning what you're worth, in love, in money, in that room where you always shrink? You might not be spiraling. Venus might just be retrograde.
[Theme music in, under]

Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy, and today Venus, the planet of love, beauty, money, and worth, has turned to walk backward for a while. So let's talk about what she is here to help us rewrite.
Segment one — what Venus retrograde actually does

About every eighteen months, Venus appears to move backward through the sky for around six weeks. And because Venus rules how we love and what we value, this is a season where all of that turns inward. Old flames resurface. Old insecurities about money and desirability get loud again. It can feel a little haunted, honestly. But the ghosts are showing up for a reason: Venus is handing you your old worth-stories so you can finally revise them.
Segment two — the stories we inherited

Here's what I mean by worth-stories. Somewhere along the way, most of us absorbed a quiet equation. "I'm lovable when I'm useful." "I'm worth more when I'm smaller." "I have to earn rest, earn love, earn taking up space." You didn't choose these. You inherited them, from culture, from family, from that one relationship that taught you to keep the peace by disappearing. Venus retrograde turns the volume up on them precisely so you can hear them clearly enough to talk back.
Your worth was never up for negotiation. You just learned to bargain with something that was always yours.
Segment three — the rewrite ritual
So grab a journal, because we're going to rewrite one story tonight.
[Music out — just voice]

Draw a line down the middle of the page. On the left, finish this sentence honestly: "I feel worthy when..." Write whatever comes, even the ugly conditional stuff. "When I'm thin." "When I'm producing." "When everyone's happy with me." Get it all out. Then, on the right side, for each one, write the truer version: "I am worthy, and..." "I am worthy, and I'm allowed to rest." "I am worthy, and I don't have to perform to be loved." Read the right side out loud. That's the rewrite. Do it as many nights as you need.
The card of the week

I pulled the Four of Cups for us, a person being offered a fresh cup they can't quite see yet because they're so focused on what's gone. Let Venus retrograde be the tap on the shoulder. Grieve what's behind you, yes. But don't miss the new love, the new worth, being offered right in front of you.
Before you go

If this cracked something open, be extra gentle with yourself these next few weeks. The free Self-Love Starter Kit is at the link to help you begin, and we do deep worth-work together inside Frequency Shift. Share this with the friend who keeps forgetting how rare she is.
[Outro music]
Until next time, remember: you don't earn your worth. You were born already holding it. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: You Are Allowed to Take Up Space
episodes.description: A short, fierce love letter for the woman who has spent too long shrinking. This is your permission slip.
showNotes: A short, fierce love letter for the woman who shrinks.
Where we first learn to make ourselves small.
A body-based practice for taking up space right now.
Your permission slip, said plainly.

transcript.x: [Cold open]
This is going to be a short one, and I need you to actually listen, not clean while you half-hear it. Put something down. This is your permission slip, and it's overdue.
[Theme music in, under]

Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy. No astrology chart today, no elaborate spread. Just a fierce little love letter for the woman who has spent way too long making herself small. If that's you, this whole episode is a hand on your shoulder.
Segment one — where we learned to shrink

Nobody is born apologizing for existing. You learned it. Maybe you were the easy child, the one who didn't cause problems, and you got praised for needing less. Maybe you loved someone whose mood was a weather system, and you got tiny to stay safe. Maybe the world just kept telling you, in a hundred small ways, that a woman is most acceptable when she takes up the least room. None of that was your fault. But un-learning it? That part is yours.
You were not born to be manageable. You were born to be here, fully.
Segment two — take up space in your body first

Here's the thing: taking up space isn't only about speaking up in meetings, though yes, do that. It starts in your body. So right now, wherever you are, if it's safe, I want you to do this with me. Uncross your arms. Uncross your legs. Roll your shoulders back and down. Take up the whole chair. Take one big breath that fills your entire torso, and let your belly actually move. Feel that? That's more of you, present, in the room. That's the whole practice. You just did it.
Segment three — your permission slip

So let me say the thing plainly, so you can borrow my voice until you find your own. You are allowed to take up space. You're allowed to have needs, and to name them. You're allowed to be too much for people who wanted less. You're allowed to be loud in your joy, big in your dreams, and unmissable in a room. You do not have to shrink so that someone else can feel tall.
Before you go

That's it. That's the episode. Short and true. If you needed it, send it to another woman who's been playing small, you both deserve to hear it. The free Self-Love Starter Kit is at the link, and Frequency Shift is where we practice this out loud, together.
[Outro music]
Until next time, take up your space, beautiful. The world adjusted around your smallness, and it will adjust around your fullness too. I love you. Bye for now.

episodes.title: New Moon Intentions & the Star Card
episodes.description: Under a dark sky, we plant seeds. The Star card lights the way toward hope, healing, and quiet renewal.
showNotes: How to set new-moon intentions that actually stick.
The Star card as your guide to hope after the fall.
A guided intention-setting ritual borrowing the Star's energy.
This week's card: the Star, and what she really means.

transcript.x: [Cold open]
The sky is dark tonight. No moon at all. And I know a dark sky can feel like an ending. But in the rhythm of the moon, this is the exact moment we plant. Let's talk about beginning again.
[Theme music in, under]

Hey love, welcome back to iLuvBnMe. I'm Stefy, and tonight we are under a new moon, which is my favorite time to dream on purpose. We're going to set intentions that actually stick, with a little help from the most hopeful card in the deck: the Star.
Segment one — what a new moon is for

Where the full moon is about release, the new moon is about planting. The sky goes dark, and in that darkness, we get to begin. It's a natural reset, a monthly invitation to ask: what do I want to grow between now and the next full moon? Not my whole five-year plan. Just this cycle. Small enough to tend, real enough to matter.
Segment two — intentions that actually stick

Here's where most intention-setting goes wrong: we write a wish list and call it a day. "I want more money. I want love. I want peace." Beautiful, but the universe deals in verbs, not wishes. So we're going to phrase our intentions as present-tense, embodied statements. Not "I want to feel calm," but "I am creating a life with more quiet in it." Not "I want love," but "I am becoming someone who receives love easily." Feel the difference? One is begging. The other is deciding.
An intention isn't a wish you send away. It's a promise you make to the woman you're becoming.
Segment three — the ritual, with the Star
So here's our new-moon ritual, and the Star is going to hold our hand through it.
[Music out — just voice]

Light a candle. Picture the Star card if you know it: that woman pouring water under a bright sky, undefended, full of quiet hope. That's the energy we're borrowing. Take three breaths. Now write three intentions for this cycle, present tense, embodied, the way we just practiced. When you've written them, read each one aloud and, like the Star, imagine pouring a little water on it. Then say: "I plant these in trust. I don't have to see the whole path to take the next step." Fold the paper, tuck it somewhere you'll find it at the full moon, and let it grow in the dark.
The card of the week

The Star, obviously, is our card, but I want to leave you with what she really means. She comes right after the Tower, the card of collapse. So if you're setting intentions from the rubble of a hard season, you are in exactly the right place. The Star is proof that hope always gets the page right after the fall.
Before you go

If you set your intentions along with me tonight, I'd love to hear them, and I'd love to keep tending them with you. The free Self-Love Starter Kit is at the link to get you started, and inside Frequency Shift we set and tend our intentions together every single cycle. Share this with the friend who's ready for a fresh start.
[Outro music]
Until next time, plant something in the dark and trust it. That is where every good thing begins. I love you. Bye for now.
